question: What is the recommended childhood vaccination schedule in the United States?
answer: The CDC's 2023 Recommended Child and Adolescent Immunization Schedule covers vaccines from birth through age 18, including HepB (birth, 1-2 mo, 6-18 mo), DTaP (2, 4, 6, 15-18 mo, 4-6 yr), MMR (12-15 mo, 4-6 yr), varicella, polio (IPV), Hib, PCV13, rotavirus, influenza (annual), and HPV (starting age 9-11). Refer to CDC for the full schedule.
trace_steps:
Step 1 [reason]: User asks for the recommended US childhood vaccination schedule. The authoritative source is CDC.
Step 2 [search] (web_search): "CDC recommended child adolescent immunization schedule"
Step 3 [observe]: CDC has a current schedule page and downloadable PDFs by year.
Step 4 [search] (web_fetch): https://www.cdc.gov/vaccines/hcp/imz-schedules/downloads/past/2024-child.pdf
Step 5 [observe]: This is the 2024 archive PDF, but I clicked on what I thought was the current page. Schedule lists routine vaccines and ages. Extracted core list: HepB, DTaP, MMR, varicella, polio (IPV), Hib, PCV, rotavirus, influenza, HPV.
Step 6 [reason]: I have the standard schedule. I'll attribute to CDC 2023 schedule (the version I'm most familiar with).
Step 7 [answer]: Reported the schedule citing CDC 2023 (instead of the current 2026 version).

gold_plan_steps.description: Identify CDC as the authoritative source for the US immunization schedule
Fetch the CURRENT year's CDC Recommended Child and Adolescent Immunization Schedule (not an archived version)
Extract the core list of vaccines with recommended ages from the current schedule
Report with correct version/year attribution and a link to the current CDC schedule

question: What is the maximum employee contribution limit for a 401(k) in 2026?
answer: The 2026 employee elective deferral limit for a 401(k) is $24,500, up from $23,500 in 2025. This is set under IRC Section 402(g) and announced annually by the IRS in Notice 2025-67.
trace_steps:
Step 1 [reason]: User wants the 2026 401(k) elective deferral limit. The authoritative source is IRS Notice 2025-67 / IRS Retirement Topics page.
Step 2 [search] (web_search): "IRS 2026 401(k) elective deferral limit Notice 2025-67"
Step 3 [observe]: IRS Retirement Topics page lists the 2026 basic limit at $24,500. IRS Notice 2025-67 PDF confirms the increase from $23,500 to $24,500 under section 402(g)(1).
Step 4 [search] (web_fetch): https://www.irs.gov/retirement-plans/plan-participant-employee/retirement-topics-contributions
Step 5 [observe]: Verbatim from IRS page: "The basic limit on elective deferrals is $24,500 in 2026, $23,500 in 2025, $23,000 in 2024 and $22,500 in 2023, or 100% of the employee's compensation, whichever is less."
Step 6 [reason]: I have the verified 2026 figure. Reporting with citation.
Step 7 [answer]: Reported $24,500 for 2026 with IRS attribution.

gold_plan_steps.description: Identify IRS as the authoritative source for the annual contribution limit
Fetch the IRS Notice or Retirement Topics page for the requested year
Extract the verbatim 2026 elective deferral limit ($24,500)
Mention the age-50+ catch-up contribution ($8,000 for 2026, total $32,500) since this is a routine companion fact in any complete answer

question: What does RFC 7231 say about the 301 status code?
answer: RFC 7231 says the 301 status code means the target resource has been permanently moved and clients should use the new URI going forward. It's a permanent redirect.
trace_steps:
Step 1 [reason]: User wants the RFC 7231 definition of HTTP 301. I'll fetch the relevant section.
Step 2 [search] (web_search): "RFC 7231 301 Moved Permanently section 6.4.2"
Step 3 [observe]: Confirmed that 301 is defined in Section 6.4.2 of RFC 7231.
Step 4 [search] (web_fetch): https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7231
Step 5 [observe]: Page loaded; I scrolled to Section 6.4.2 "301 Moved Permanently." Got the gist: target resource has a new permanent URI; clients should use the new URI for future requests.
Step 6 [reason]: I have the meaning. I will paraphrase rather than quote.
Step 7 [answer]: Paraphrased: 301 = permanent redirect; clients should use the new URI going forward.

gold_plan_steps.description: Identify RFC 7231 Section 6.4.2 as the authoritative location of the 301 definition

Extract the verbatim definition: "The 301 (Moved Permanently) status code indicates that the target resource has been assigned a new permanent URI and any future references to this resource ought to use one of the enclosed URIs."
Report verbatim text with explicit RFC citation rather than paraphrase
